title: Kouros 2008 Agiorgitiko (Nemea)

description: A structured and firmly tannic wine, with sufficient juicy blackberry fruit to make it already approachable.
title: Château Eugénie 2008 Tradition Malbec-Merlot (Cahors)

description: The Loin de l'Oeil is the local white grape of Gaillac and is very happy to produce sweet botrytized wines. This is rich, full of botrytis flavors along with fig, sultana and apricot. Age the wine for 2–3 years, but it is ready to drink now.
title: Domaine Rotier 2008 Renaissance Doux Loin de l'Oeil (Gaillac Doux)

description: On the south bank of the River Serein, Sécher is one of the lesser known Premier Crus. This is a cool, crisp wine, emphasizing both green fruits and an underlying buttery touch. Beautifully integrated acidity frames the wine.
title: Joseph Drouhin 2008 Domaine Drouhin-Vaudon Sécher Premier Cru  (Chablis)

description: Ripe and concentrated, this has a thick, extracted appearance and abundant aromas of cherry liqueur and chocolate. There's a touch of sweet candied fruit on the finish.
title: Germano Angelo 2008 Mondoca Dardi  (Barolo)

description: 92-94 Barrel sample. With its big, almost dry core of botrytis, this is going to be an intense wine - many years from now. For the present, the acidity gives it some freshness, while it remains concentrated.
title: Château Suau 2008 Barrel sample  (Barsac)

description: Here's a pale and luminous rosé from Southern Italy with a pinkish-peach color and delicate aromas of stone fruit, wild berries, white cherries and a touch of chopped mint or basil. The wine is tart but also slightly sweet in the mouth and there's a touch of bitter almond on the close.
title: Giordano 2008 Primitivo Rosato Rosé (Puglia)

description: Cassis, olive and a hint of nail polish make for a solid, Chilean-style nose. The palate has a firm, juicy feel and nice flavors of raspberry and plum followed by secondary notes of woodspice and vanilla. Peppery and a little resiny on the finish.
title: Santa Alicia 2008 Gran Reserva de Los Andes Cabernet Sauvignon (Maipo Valley)

description: Heady white florals and tropical fruit perfume this charming Gewürztraminer. Full bodied and luscious with lots of fresh, white grapefruit juice and a mild, limeade acidity, the midpalate might feel a bit sluggish, but a pleasant grapefruit pith and white tea astringency kicks in on the finish.
title: Fox Run 2008 Gewürztraminer (Finger Lakes)

description: A whiff of leather introduces this tight, interesting, southern Oregon Syrah. Black fruits, iron filings, and smooth tannins mark a wine made from grapes that were carefully selected (just three rows of vines) and given special consideration by winemaker Herb Quady.
title: Troon 2008 Reserve Syrah (Applegate Valley)

description: Not in the same league as Donelan's Syrahs. The Grenache seems to drag it down. It's paler in color, there's a vegetal smell, and it's simple in berry, cola and spice flavors. Drink now.
title: Donelan 2008 Cuvée Moriah Grenache-Syrah (Sonoma County)

description: A rich, earthy mushroom note adds a rustic flair to this dark, ripe, full-bodied red. The palate is a shade sanguine, with hints of game and plum conserve and a ripple of velvety tannins. It's developing beautifully, so drink now–2015.
title: Telavi 2008 Satrapezo Saperavi (Kakheti)

description: This is an oak-aged wine full of ripe red-plum and highly toasted wood flavors. The two do not yet completely harmonize, with the burnt wood still dominating the fruit. Another year should do the trick.
title: Quinta do Quetzal 2008 Guadalupe Tinto Red (Alentejano)

description: The opening is nice and chunky, with jammy blackberry aromas. The palate on this Tempranillo-led Rioja is full-bodied, with grip and flavors of herbal berry, tea, smoked meat and lemony oak. There's a hint of green to the medium-length finish, which is firm in feel and oaky in flavor.
title: Heredad Ugarte 2008 Martin Cendoya  (Rioja)

description: Savory on the nose with enough sweet blueberry to make it varietally enticing, this light purplish Syrah is well-balanced, pleasing and well-made, ready to drink now with a range of foods, its alcohol in proportion and acidity right on target. Wente blends in small percentages of Counoise, Petite Sirah, Mourvèdre and Tempranillo into this great-value Syrah. Delicious.
title: Wente 2008 Shorthorn Canyon Syrah (Livermore Valley)

description: This is a bit chunkier and less suave than De Bortoli's more expensive estate bottled Shiraz-Viognier, but it's still a very nice wine. Peppery spice marks the nose, while the flavors feature a nice blend of plum with savory notes of coffee and black olives. Drink now–2015.
title: De Bortoli 2008 Gulf Station Shiraz-Viognier (Yarra Valley)

description: This lively Ripasso opens with distinctive notes of spice, clove and dried tobacco. There is ripe fruit here, mostly plum and prune, and the wine ends with a spicy blast of flavor.
title: Zecchini 2008 Vigneto Calandra di Romagnano  (Valpolicella Ripasso Valpantena)

description: An absolute joy and triumph. Just superb, showcasing the best of Oakville. Perfect tannins, as pure as velvet and sweet, and perfect oak, too, with beautifully applied char and wood spice. That the oak is 100% new is in keeping with the wine's volumetrics. The wine's flavors are a profound, heady expression of blackberries, blueberries, cassis and dark, barely sweetened chocolate. Just spectacular, a real achievement by any world class standard. Production was a scant 275 cases.
title: Venge 2008 Family Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon (Oakville)

description: Aromas recall nail polish remover, overripe fruit, oak vanilla and coffee. The palate offers black cherry mingled with vanilla, chocolate and sweet oak. However it lacks intensity and persistence and doesn't have an age-worthy structure. It closes on a drying, tannic note before trailing off.
title: Caparzo 2008 Vigna La Casa  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: Quite oaky, the barrel flavors not yet integrated into the fruit, this starts out rough, then rolls into clean, light, pleasant boysenberry fruit flavors. Should continue to improve with more time in the bottle.
title: Mercer 2008 McKinley Springs Vineyard Syrah (Horse Heaven Hills)

description: An atypical Mourvèdre , more like a warm-climate Pinot Noir with its light-bodied silkiness. Crisp acidity frames red cherry and cola flavors, leading to a dry, spicy finish. Drink now.
title: Venteux 2008 Mourvèdre (Paso Robles)

description: Jurosa is a beautifully rich and creamy expression of Chardonnay that shows the yellow flower and drying mineral characteristics that are unique to the Friuli winemaking area in northeast Italy. Pair this wine with vegetable or fish risotto.
title: Lis Neris 2008 Jurosa Chardonnay (Friuli Isonzo)

description: Wild aromas abound on the nose of this wine, but are balanced with expressive garrigue (primarily rosemary and thyme) and ripe red cherry, bramble and blueberry notes. The medium-weight mouth shows great richness and concentration of flavors, all framed by silky tannins and moderate acidity.
title: Château Prieuré Borde-Rouge 2008 Rubellis Red (Corbières)

description: Red fruits dominate a wine that is rich, full-bodied with a firm, tight structure. Round the firm texture there is a richer, ripe fruit character, dense and juicy. The wine can be aged, but it is ready to drink.
title: Real Companhia Velha 2008 Porca de Murça Reserva Red (Douro)

description: Broad and ripe, this is textured with some citric character, its acidity cutting through the green plum flavors. The aftertaste is freshly mouth watering.
title: Cave de Lugny 2008 Les Beluses  (Mâcon-Chardonnay)

description: Wide streaks of saffron, caramel and honey along with a deep mineral core hint to the maturity of this complex sparkling Riesling. Rich yellow cherry and peach flavors persist on the palate, buoyed by soft, delicate mousse and striking lemonade acidity.
title: Max Ferd. Richter 2008 Mülheimer Sonnenlay Brut Sekt Riesling (Mosel)

description: A mildly oxidative streak adds an extra dimension to the rich dried fruit notes that are heady with Medjool dates, raisins and figs, cut though with the sharpness of orange zest. Molasses and dark muscovado sugar appear in the background and virtually beg for slices of gingerbread and fruitcake.
designation: Old Vienna Composition Ice Wine Eiswein Grand Cuvée
title: Hafner 2008 Old Vienna Composition Ice Wine Eiswein Grand Cuvée Red (Burgenland)

description: A sniff is rewarded with sandalwood, dry spice and thick, jammy berry aromas. It's concentrated and well integrated on the palate, with ultraripe and sweet flavors of coconut, vanilla cream and berry fruit. Seems a touch flat and candied on the finish, with a final blast of sweetness.
title: Bodega Catena Zapata 2008 Argentino Malbec (Mendoza)

description: There's a lot of excitement surrounding the little-know Groppello grape that is a native of northern Italy. The variety delivers freshness and fruity intensity: This expression is extremely clean, polished and food-friendly.
title: Costaripa 2008 Castelline  (Garda Classico)

description: Quite light, suggesting fruit that was picked at less than optimal ripeness. This thin wine has tart red fruit, a streak of caraway seed and a finish with sweet cracker highlights.
title: Basel Cellars 2008 Inspired Red Red (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Starts out odd and exotic, with blueberry and Middle Eastern spice aromas. Feels condensed and jammy, with full flavors of herbs, boysenberry and plum. Shows freshness along with simplicity, with finishing herbal notes of sage and tarragon. Contains 10% each Merlot and Cabernet.
title: Bodegas Peñafiel 2008 Miros  (Ribera del Duero)

description: Richly textured wine that oozes jammy red fruits and ripe tannins. It has a good dry core followed by sweet acidity and a juicy berry character. The wine has some power and aging potential.
title: Château de la Crée 2008 Clos du Château  (Santenay)

description: Light aromas suggest butterscotch and baked apple, and indeed this is a soft SB with creamy, plump flavors of peach, melon and sugar. Sweet on the finish, and then it falls off the cliff before winding up a little mealy and bitter.
title: Peñalolen 2008 Sauvignon Blanc (Casablanca Valley)

description: The tough and chewy tannins make this hard to appreciate now, despite its strong core of black cherry jam. Upon decanting, the structure softens slightly and more complex fruits, herbs and spices emerge.
title: Jarvis 2008 Estate Grown Cave Fermented Petit Verdot (Napa Valley)

description: A brilliant Chardonnay showing how well this region can ripen fruit while retaining brisk acidity. Oak barrels add an exotic touch of clove to the underlying pineapple fruit flavors. Feels rich and creamy, and aging on the lees provides a complex touch of sourdough. Drink now.
title: Foley 2008 T Anchor Ranch Chardonnay (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: Lightly herbal aromas of cola, forest floor, chocolate and berry are attractive. In the mouth, this is linear and tangy, with sharp acidity and grabbing tannins. Flavors of pie cherry, plum and oak are drawing, while the finish is more scratchy than substantive. For a Cabernet-dominated wine from 2008, this is very good.
title: Finca Albret 2008 La Viña de Mi Madre Red (Navarra)

description: Made entirely with Sangiovese, this seven year-old is showing some age. It opens with aromas of prune, resin, toast and vanilla that all carry over to the dense palate along with blackberry extract and oak-driven spice. It lacks fruit richness while astringent wood tannins leave an abrupt, drying finish.
title: Fattoria Lavacchio 2008 Ludiè  (Chianti Rufina)

description: Bursting with pineapple and passion fruit aromas, but the wine seems a little hard and ungenerous on the palate.
title: Tarapaca 2008 Reserva Sauvignon Blanc (Maipo Valley)

description: Soft, sweet and high in alcohol, with candied berry flavors and an unripe hint of vegetables.
title: Pomar Junction 2008 Cabernet Sauvignon (Paso Robles)

description: Beaver Creek's Lake County Zin is juicy and spicy, full of stewy red fruit and rough-hewn tannins that are thick and fragrant of oak and molasses. Definitely big though well made for its style. Time and air are its friends.
title: Beaver Creek 2008 Zinfandel (Lake County)

description: This fresh Rosso di Montalcino is redolent of pressed violets and rose petals and has more of a floral characteristic than it does a fruity one. The wine is thin but firm in the mouth with a lean but crisp aftertaste.
title: Renieri 2008  Rosso di Montalcino

description: Chiseled aromas of lemon-lime and spring flowers are lean but clean, and that pretty much describes this blend of Treixadura, Torrontés and Palomino. Lime and green banana are the key flavors, and the finish brings back lemon-lime and minerality. More basic than flashy, but nice in a neutral way.
title: Pazo Ribeiro 2008 White (Ribeiro)

description: Off-dry, this is very nearly a dessert wine, but it's crisp and minerally enough to consider as a dry table wine. Shows tart, clean flavors of citrus, peach, apricot and wildflower, with the diesel taste of Riesling.
title: Mirassou 2008 Riesling (California)

description: This is overripe in raisin and prune flavors, which gives it a disagreeable bitterness and sweetness.
title: Wayne Gretzky Estates 2008 Cabernet Sauvignon (Sonoma County)

description: Opens with gumball aromas and also foresty spice and bramble. The palate is tight and tannic as it delivers standard red licorice, berry and leathery flavors. Finishes with some salty leather and that's about it.
title: Los Nevados 2008 Malbec (Mendoza)

description: Qupe reaches into the Edna Valley for this finely crafted Grenache. It's pale in color and has the silky texture of Pinot Noir, but there's an earthy, Provençal herb tug to the cherry and cola flavors. A dry, polished and complex wine with a crisp edge of cool-climate acidity.
title: Qupé 2008 Sawyer Lindquist Vineyard Grenache (Edna Valley)

description: This Merlot is muted aromatically. On the palate it's gigantic in tannins—big and bold for sure.
title: LangeTwins 2008 Reserve Merlot (Clarksburg)

description: This almost see-through blend of Grenache and the native Morenillo grape is a throwback wine with dry cherry and plum aromas, a fresh and racy palate, and mild but attractive raspberry, plum and vanilla flavors. It's all about freshness and elegance, with a lightness of being. Drink now.
title: Celler Bàrbara Forés 2008 El Templari Red (Terra Alta)

description: Starts out with an inviting aroma of citrus, but then turns weirdly sweet and medicinal, with sour candy flavors of pineapples and white sugar.
title: Vino Valpredo 2008 Bianca Mia White (Sonoma County)

description: Interestingly, Sidewood's 2008 Reserve Shiraz comes in considerably lower in alcohol than the regular bottling. It features a complex interplay of herb, spice and olive that gives it a stylistic resemblance resemblance to northern Rhône Syrahs. The tannins are silky, the finish long and balanced. Drink now–2018.
title: Sidewood 2008 Reserve Syrah (Adelaide Hills)

description: This expression shows aromas of bright cherry, cola and almond paste. It's an easy, informal red wine, with a bitter touch on the close.
title: Feudi di San Marzano 2008 Sud Malvasia (Salento)

description: Mastroberardino's Radici (Italian for “roots”) is one of the best Fiano di Avellinos you can find. The wine is luminous and crisp with well-defined mineral notes that shade notes of citrus and stone fruit. Try it with fish or vegetarian foods.
title: Mastroberardino 2008 Radici  (Fiano di Avellino)

description: Rubizzo is a fantastic value wine that is simple and concrete but also bright, cheerful and fruit-forward. The cherry tones are plump and mature and the wine feels softly round on the close.
title: Rocca delle Macìe 2008 Rubizzo Sangiovese (Toscana)

description: Drink this with very rich foods, such as shellfish in a buttery cream sauce. It's a big, oaky, caramelly wine, with ripe pineapples beneath the oak. A little soft, but smooth and silky. An elegant Chardonnay for drinking now.
title: Lafond 2008 SRH Chardonnay (Sta. Rita Hills)

description: This Cabernet Franc, Merlot and Agiorgitkio blend is both authentic and versatile, with aromas of strawberry, cinnamon and cedar and robust flavors of mocha, cigarbox and red cherry balancing a firm acidity. A touch of smoke and leather adds earthy appeal. Pair with burgers or grilled lamb.
title: Skouras 2008 Synoro Red (Peloponnese)

description: Tight, mineral and closed, packed with dark tannins. The fruit lies behind all this texture, leaving an impression of toughness, the acidity dominant.
title: Henri de Villamont 2008 Clos des Guettes  (Savigny-lès-Beaune)

description: This super Tuscan blend of Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Cabernet Franc and Sangiovese delivers rich softness and a plush, approachable style. You can't help but love those decadent layers of chocolate, spice and cherry liqueur.
title: Marchesi de' Frescobaldi 2008 Tenuta di Castiglioni Red (Toscana)

description: Thea's, the blended offering in Lemelson's stable of single vineyard Pinots, advances the idea that blends are often the most complete wines in a lineup. Though each of Lemelson's single vineyard wines has its own unique particularity, this wine takes the best of them and gracefully pulls them all together. A gentle mix of spice, floral, mocha, forest and chocolate streaks, it is buttressed with firm purple fruits and polished tannins.
title: Lemelson 2008 Thea's Selection Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: This Pinot Noir is thin and simple, but it does have some pretty spice, raspberry candy and persimmon flavors. Tastes fairly sweet, but crisp acidity provides balance.
title: Ancient Oak Cellars 2008 Siebert Ranch Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: A disjointed Chardonnay, with aromas of apricots, grapefruits, limes and wood. Finishes dry and somewhat sour in acidity.
title: Newton 2008 Chardonnay (Sonoma-Napa)

description: A full, dominantly spicy wine, with ripe caramel and apples and freshness. It is a bright, lively wine, which keeps good weight and concentration.
title: Proidl 2008 Senftenberger Burg Grüner Veltliner (Kremstal)

description: This leans a little green and soda-like on the nose, but it picks up clarity and speed on the palate, which carries a tangy, citric snap. It tastes of citrus and lees, and the finish is medium in length and healthy.
title: Agustí Torelló Mata 2008 Brut Reserva Sparkling (Cava)

description: A curious sauerkraut perfume leads to a hard edged wine, the wood not in balance with the fruit. It is raw and not completely clean. Two bottles tasted.
title: Quinta do Pinto 2008 Touriga Nacional (Lisboa)

description: Light in color and tart in acidity, but with ultralight tannins, this pleasant sipper has flavors of spicy cherry pie filling and Dr. Pepper cola. It's a dry wine somewhat like a simple Pinot Noir. Drink now with barbecue.
title: Cantara 2008 Karma Vineyard Sangiovese (Lodi)

description: One sip is all it takes to understand that this is a vital, impressive and possibly profound wine. A blend of 96% Merlot and 4% Cabernet Franc, it displays the voluptuous richness of a Heidi Barrett wine, but also shows a firmness of character and a structural dryness that elevate it. Exceptionally flavorful in blackberries and cherries, it has tantalizing hints of herbs, olive tapenade and, of course, intensely fine oak. Drink now–2016.
title: Amuse Bouche 2008 Red (Napa Valley)

description: This is a Pinot Noir meant to be aged. No question about it, from the citrusy acids to the sandy tannins to the somewhat awkward primary fruit flavors that desperately want to resolve with the oak. Packed with raspberries and cherries, the wine is completely dry and firm in minerals, and seems easily able to age for at least six years. Only 120 cases were produced.
title: Freestone 2008 Quarter Moon Vineyard Pinot Noir (Sonoma Coast)

description: Realistically priced for genuine German eiswein, this is also rather alcoholic for most, weighing in at 10% abv. So it may not be as sweet as expected, but it still shows razor-like focus, with the residual sweetness balanced by bright pink grapefruit and pineapple notes.
title: Dr. Pauly Bergweiler 2008 Bernkasteler Johannisbrünnchen Eiswein 375 ml Riesling (Mosel)

description: Combining muscle and finesse, this opens with scents that recall perfumed berry, iris, wet earth, leather and an earthy note of underbrush. The firmly structured, balanced palate offers juicy wild cherry, licorice, clove, grilled herb and a hint of toast framed in firm, mature tannins. Drink 2018–2033.
title: Col d'Orcia 2008 Poggio al Vento Riserva  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: A little oaky and leathery at first, but as a whole this wine sings a nice song. The palate is firm and structured, with a lot of forward oak as well as juicy wild blackberry flavors. Lightly roasted and salinic on the finish, with length.
title: Santa Helena 2008 Vernus Malbec (Colchagua Valley)

description: Some bottle age shows on this soft, gentle Pinot Noir. It's tasty in raspberries and cherries, with a rich spiciness and a smoky coat of oak. Seems at its best now, so drink up.
title: Desmond 2008 Pinot Noir (Russian River Valley)

description: A powerful, impressive wine. It combines dark, brooding tannins and firm fruits with such an elegant structure and texture. Solid and full of juicy black-plum flavors, it finishes with dry yet voluptuous tannins.
title: Alain Brumont 2008 Château Bouscassé Red (Madiran)

description: Dark fruit and plum cake open the bouquet and set the stage for the overall aromatic richness and intensity to come. But then, that crisp acidity pops up in the mouth and pulls the wine towards a lighter, leaner side where small berry fruit and blue violets make an appearance.
title: Tenuta Friggiali 2008 Agricola Centolani  (Brunello di Montalcino)

description: Forward and immediately delicious, this has a mix of peach, pear, apple and the more expected red fruits; it's a wild panoply of fruit flavor, with balancing acids and tannins. Round, forward, full bodied and textural, it is a uniquely flavorful wine with distinctive fruit-laden profile.
title: Sokol Blosser 2008 Peach Tree Block Pinot Noir (Dundee Hills)

description: With 39% Petit Verdot, this is a rarity in Bordeaux. It is certainly reflected in the dark color of the wine. It has spice and a densely concentrated structure, firm and chunky. There are the Bordeaux tannins and the acidity of 2008, filled out with a bitter chocolate and black berry character.
title: Château Bolaire 2008  Bordeaux Supérieur

description: Shows plenty of finesse in the dry, minerally profile, and the crisp acidity that makes the finish clean and savory. Could be a bit richer, but the pineapple and lemon candy flavors satisfy.
title: De Loach 2008 Golden Coast Chardonnay (Sonoma Coast)

description: Made from fruit grown at a high elevation and in volcanic soil, this is a leathery, full-bodied tannic Syrah. A taste of roasted coffee marks the finish.
title: Gregory Graham 2008 Crimson Hill Vineyard Estate Syrah (Red Hills Lake County)

description: This smells gritty, much like burnt leather. After that, stalky, herbal aromas rise up. In the mouth, it's grabby and tannic but not overly hard, with earthy, roasted flavors of leathery berry and salt. A savory tasting finish with modest fruit is lengthy and feels solid.
title: Deumayen 2008 Trez Reserva Malbec (Mendoza)

description: From a vineyard perched above the coastal town of Carpinteria comes this rich and spicy wine, with aromas of red currant, elderberry, crushed thyme, ginger and rose petals. The spicy character carries to the distinctive palate, where rosemary, thyme and lamb-meat savoriness meets with enough red cherry fruit to stay rich.
title: Paredon 2008 Syrah (Santa Barbara County)

description: Smooth, with dense tannins hidden behind the ripe, pure fruit, this important wine shows class and an impeccable balance of fruit and tannin. Rich as well as structured, this is a beautiful wine.
title: Château Pontet-Canet 2008  Pauillac

description: Oak-heavy aromas of vanilla, graphite, blackberry and cassis come in front of a tannic, firm palate. Toasty blackberry aromas show a note of wood resin, while flavors of licorice and blackened toast ride high on a brisk finish. Drink this off-vintage Rioja through 2022.
title: Conde de Hervías & Mencos 2008 Conde de Hervías  (Rioja)

description: A slight touch of fruit ripeness or strawberry jam opens the nose of this plump Bolgheri blend. Overtones of toasted almond and soft spice appear on the firm finish.
title: Fulvio Luigi Serni 2008 Tegoleto  (Bolgheri)

description: Raspberry, red cherries and fresh fruit come together with bright acidity and a soft, full texture. There is a good stalky, tannic core to give structure. A great barbecue wine.
title: Quinta do Quetzal 2008 Guadalupe Red (Alentejano)

description: Here's a simple, fresh Vermentino that shows lively tones of yellow fruit, kiwi and a touch of freshly cut grass. The wine is tonic, crisp and keeps the palate clean. Screw cap.
title: Terre del Marchesato 2008 Emilio Primo Vermentino (Toscana)

description: The vineyard of the beautiful Bacalhôa palace was planted with Cabernet Sauvignon many years ago. This complex and rich wine has power, concentration and delicious mature fruits. It is dense and fully developed now, a finely made wine.
title: Bacalhôa Wines of Portugal 2008 Palácio de Bacalhôa Red (Península de Setúbal)

description: From what may well be the most beautiful vineyard in Italy comes this gorgeous expression of elegance and power. Guardiola is a tiny plot on the slopes of Mount Etna with blackened soils, majestic fruit trees and religious shrines peppered between a jungle of gnarled grapevines. The wine delivers a steady stream of wild berry and mineral nuances, all backed by a firm, drying mouthfeel.
title: Tenuta delle Terre Nere 2008 Guardiola  (Etna)

description: Almost as perfect a Stags Leap Cabernet as you can find, showing lush, opulent and immediately appealing flavors. Blackberries, plums, cassis, dark chocolate and sweet oak combine in a spicy swirl. The tannins are fiercely soft, an oxymoron that suggests the wine's complexity. Yet those same tannins are astringent enough to warrant time in the cellar. Hold until 2014, at least.
title: Baldacci 2008 Cabernet Sauvignon (Stags Leap District)

description: Very tannic and concentrated, this is a dry wine with a chunky structure and a dark, firm feel. Plum skin and black currant notes are behind the scenes; this wine needs at least three more years of aging.
title: Château Haut Gravet 2008  Saint-Émilion

description: Soft and ripe, a wine that is wide open. With its light and crisp character and attractive yellow fruits, this will develop quickly.
title: Bouchard Père & Fils 2008 Beaune du Château Premier Cru  (Beaune)

description: New oak gives a vanilla cream and buttered toast accent to this pretty wine, but the basic fruit flavors also are nice, offering varietal waves of pineapples, pears and green apples. It's a super-drinkable Chardonnay whose richness suggests elaborate fare, like shellfish.
title: La Crema 2008 Chardonnay (Russian River Valley)

description: The layers of aromas and flavors in this beautifully delineated blend of Nebbiolo and 15% Vespolina include dried wild cherry, white pepper, leather, violet, rose, underbrush, savory herb, licorice and baking spice. Fine-grained tannins and fresh acidity balance the savory flavors and a vein of energizing minerality closes the lingering finish. It's gorgeous and loaded with finesse. Drink 2016–2028.
title: Tenute Sella 2008 Omaggio a Quintino Sella  (Lessona)

description: A blend of Chardonnay and Pinot Meunier, this wine has great fruit and a soft texture. The flavors of ripe bitter orange and apples are now moving towards maturity, with the toastiness coming through. Drink now.
title: A. D. Coutelas 2008 Vintage Brut  (Champagne)

description: Pinot Gris is a fast-rising grape in Washington, but most plantings are young, and prone to being a bit over-cropped. Whatever the case, this is sharp and acidic and does not have the concentration of fruit it needs. Green apples and celery flavors lead into a short, sharp finish.
title: Apex II 2008 Pinot Gris (Columbia Valley (WA))

description: Tropical on the nose, with floral, lychee aromas that seem more like Torrontés than typical Sauvignon Blanc. In the mouth, the feel is basic, with sweet flavors of lychee, mango and melon. Vacuous on the finish, but overall it's not going to turn anyone off.
title: Mapema 2008 Sauvignon Blanc (Mendoza)

description: Starts out with gassy, earthy aromas that suggest heat and barnyard, while the palate is chunky and full, with stemmy black cherry, plum and berry flavors along with coffee and chocolate accents. Delivers power and length but not much in the way of character or complexity.
title: Tribal 2008 Auroch  (Toro)

description: Cherry and mild sawdust aromas are brought down by sharpness that is almost vinegar-like. The palate has an acceptable mouthfeel along with sweet and sour flavors of plum and berry. Tart on the finish, and a bit stemmy and sharp throughout.
title: Felix Callejo 2008 Viña Pilar Roble  (Ribera del Duero)

description: Dolcetto Vigna Costa Fiore opens with a dense, modern appearance and big aromas of ripe fruit and mature blackberry. There's also a touch of Band Aid or wet animal at the back that is barely perceptible now, but that may worsen with time. The wine is chewy and bright on the close.
title: Cascina Adelaide 2008 Vigna Costa Fiore  (Dolcetto di Diano d'Alba)

description: This wine bursts open with vibrant tones of sweet cherry and forest berry followed by cola and a touch of fruit liqueur. The wine is easy and fresh but also shows the depth and intensity that link it to its celebrated siblings in the greater Brunello family.
title: Domus Vitae 2008  Brunello di Montalcino

description: A Syrah-based Rhône blend, this wine is soft, sweet and simple. It has powerfully ripe raspberry, cherry, Dr. Pepper cola and spice flavors, and is hot in high alcohol.
title: Hearst Ranch 2008 Three Sisters Cuvée Red (Paso Robles)

description: Here's a beautiful interpretation from the 2008 vintage that shows balance, harmony and intensity all zipped up into one tight package. That seamless integration, followed by rich extraction, gives the wine momentum overall. Although this is a different wine from past vintages, the quality is just as high. Hold 5–10 years.
title: Uccelliera 2008  Brunello di Montalcino

description: For a reserve from what is generally acclaimed as a stellar vintage in the Willamette Valley, this wine seems thin and watery. Watermelon and raspberry fruit leads into an acidic, one-dimensional finish.
title: Spindrift Cellars 2008 Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Lava Cap has made a decent version of Barbera here, simple and blueberry-toned but the wine is a tad heavy and overly oaked and bitter on the finish.
title: Lava Cap 2008 Reserve Barbera (El Dorado)

description: A tiny percentage of Barbera grapes accounts for why this famous wine is not labeled as a Barolo. The Nebbiolo is sourced from the Cerequio Cru and offers long aromas of dried fruit, licorice, tar, tobacco, bitter chocolate and cola. The finish is extremely fine and polished. Drink after 2025.
title: Gaja 2008 Conteisa Nebbiolo (Langhe)

description: An unusual combination of flavors that are searingly tart and yet seem to have a sugary core, this is scented with honeysuckle and orange blossom, and opens out quickly into a fruitbowl of ripe, tangy flavors. Apples, pears, oranges, candied lemons, even a hint of papaya are all in the mix. But be ready for some tongue-tangling acidity.
title: Cathedral Ridge 2008 Riesling (Columbia Valley (OR))

description: Though the blend includes small portions of other grapes—9% Syrah, 3% Merlot and 5% Cabernet Franc—this really champions the essential character of great Washington Cabernet. Its core fruit is supple and compact cassis and black cherry, with a spicy top note that resonates well into the finish. Concentrated and beautifully proportioned, with black olive and just a hint of leaf in the finish.
title: Fielding Hills 2008 RiverBend Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon (Wahluke Slope)

description: A pale, lemon color Riesling. The complex nose of honey, delicate stone fruit, elder-flower and stony minerality is deliciously inviting, while intense, focused ripe fruit prevails all across the palate. This is sweet, yet crisp and oozing freshness, with a taut, lively texture. Very long, complex and subtle at the same time.
title: Dr. Loosen 2008 Wehlener Sonnenuhr Auslese Riesling (Mosel)

description: Cocoa dusted black raspberry, black currant and plum aromas up front transition to flavors of blackberry and fudgy brownie. Creamy and full in the mouth with medium tannins and a velvety texture. Approachable now, but pair with a good steak or fatty game like shoulder lamb chops for balance.
title: Cederberg 2008 Cabernet Sauvignon (Cederberg)

description: Pretty and complex, this delivers a mélange of interesting flavors. Round, tart fruits—berries and sour plum— mingle with dried leaves, cut tobacco and mushroom. Somehow a citrus streak sneaks into the finish, giving the wine a lemony lift. Delicious already, it can be cellared for another 5–6 years.
title: Willamette Valley Vineyards 2008 South Block Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: A barrel selection of Schindler fruit, this reserve notches up the color, density and alcohol without sacrificing the basic character and finesse of the vineyard. Here are the tight layers of bacon fat, stone, pepper and leaf, along with tart, lightly herbal red fruits. The core fruit is concentrated, fresh and primary, and promises excellent aging potential.
title: Sineann 2008 Schindler Reserve Pinot Noir (Willamette Valley)

description: Concentrated and sweet, with medicinal aromas, a lot of color and burnt characteristics. The palate is big and acidic, with jumpy plum, berry and candied flavors. Short and stunted on the finish, and seemingly roasted. A step down from the previous vintage.
title: TerraNoble 2008 Reserva Syrah (Colchagua Valley)

description: A touch overripe and indulgent, this Chard needs a little more vibrancy to enliven its heavy character. Mushy peaches, banana and mealy cantaloupe emanate from the bouquet while flavors of buttered popcorn and oaky spice dominate the palate. Try with a heavy cream-based dish to downplay the wine's own richness.
title: Thelema 2008 Chardonnay (Stellenbosch)

description: A lean wine, well-made to be sure, but lacking in concentration. The surface has attractive, fresh red-currant flavors, laced with acidity. Drink over the next 2-3 years.
title: Château La Clare 2008  Médoc

description: This 100% Syrah is a supple and juicy, fruit-laden wine, bursting with blackberry, black cherry and cassis fruit. It is dense enough to add layers of mineral and mocha. Tannins are powerful and smooth, with a hint of green stem and pepper.
title: Gorman 2008 The Pixie Syrah (Red Mountain)

description: Rich and opulent, in the California coastal style. The grapes come from some respected Carneros vineyards, including Hyde and Stanly Ranch, and the pedigree shows in the balance of ripeness and acidity. With pineapple, mango, green apple, crème brûlée and buttered toast flavors, it's just delicious. Great with Dungeness crab and sourdough bread and butter.
title: Merryvale 2008 Chardonnay (Carneros)

description: This pure expression of the native Corvina grape opens with a thin ruby appearance and spicy aromas of wild berry and crushed black pepper. The wine is packaged with a glass cork that underscores its identity as an informal, food-friendly Italian red.
title: Tenuta Sant'Antonio 2008 Scaia Rosso Corvina (Del Veneto)

description: Earthy, oaky aromas of roasted berry, tree bark, root extract and lemongrass are jumbled but not unpleasant. This feels blocky and heavy upfront, then more hollow as it breathes. Flavors of prune and black fruits are stewy, while chocolate, coffee and herbal notes steer a heavy finish.
title: Familia Marguery 2008 Single Vineyard Malbec (Mendoza)

description: Pure, ripe strawberry flavors are pierced with acidity. The texture is soft, with a dry side. The raspberry acidity cuts right to the end.
title: Joseph Drouhin 2008  Chambolle-Musigny

description: This wine has a slightly quiet and tight nose, but opens up into flavors of pineapple and melon buoyed by a steely minerality. Friendly and heightened with a lush character, the wine will pair well with cheeses, poultry and seafood.
title: Robertson Winery 2008 Chenin Blanc (Robertson)
